dependencies.description: Both vPC peer switches should run the same NX-OS version to ensure consistent behavior and avoid ISSU complications
dependencies.name: Matching NX-OS version on both peers

dependencies.description: A dedicated port-channel configured as a trunk carrying all vPC VLANs, used for CFS synchronization and data forwarding
dependencies.name: Peer-link (port-channel with trunk)

dependencies.description: A separate Layer 3 path (management VRF or dedicated link) for UDP keepalive messages used in dual-active detection
dependencies.name: Peer-keepalive link

dependencies.description: Both peers must be configured with the same vPC domain ID
dependencies.name: Matching vPC domain ID

dependencies.description: All vPC member port-channels must use LACP (active mode) for reliable multi-chassis link aggregation
dependencies.name: LACP on all vPC member ports

failure_modes.description: The peer-link port-channel has failed, breaking CFS synchronization. The secondary switch suspends its vPC member ports to prevent dual-active forwarding.

resolutions.action: Verify peer-link port-channel members: 'show port-channel summary' — check all member links

Verify peer-link STP state: 'show spanning-tree vlan <peer-link-vlans>' — must be forwarding

Check physical cabling and optics on peer-link members

If configuration error, compare peer-link config on both switches
If configuration error, compare peer-link config on both switches
If configuration error, compare peer-link config on both switches

root_causes.cause: All member links of peer-link port-channel failed
STP blocking peer-link (should never happen — peer-link must be STP forwarding)
Configuration error on peer-link

symptoms: show vpc shows peer-link as down
Secondary switch suspends vPC member ports
Traffic from hosts dual-homed to secondary is disrupted

failure_modes.description: Hosts connected to orphan ports (single-attached to one vPC switch) lose connectivity when the peer-link fails on the secondary switch, because the secondary suspends all vPCs and SVIs.

resolutions.action: Identify orphan ports: 'show vpc orphan-ports'
Identify orphan ports: 'show vpc orphan-ports'
Identify orphan ports: 'show vpc orphan-ports'

Configure 'vpc orphan-ports suspend' on the secondary's orphan port interfaces so they suspend and hosts fail over to an alternate path, or ensure orphan ports are on the primary switch

failure_modes.description: After a vPC peer reloads in a VXLAN EVPN fabric, traffic destined to the anycast VTEP (VIP) may be black-holed because the underlay and overlay have not yet converged, but the NVE interface is already up and attracting traffic.

resolutions.action: Tune 'delay restore <1-3600>' under vpc domain (default 150s)
Tune 'delay restore <1-3600>' under vpc domain (default 150s)
Tune 'delay restore <1-3600>' under vpc domain (default 150s)
Tune 'delay restore orphan-port <0-300>' under vpc domain

Tune 'delay restore orphan-port <0-300>' under vpc domain
Tune 'delay restore interface-vlan <1-3600>' under vpc domain for anycast gateway SVIs
Tune 'delay restore interface-vlan <1-3600>' under vpc domain for anycast gateway SVIs
Tune 'delay restore interface-vlan <1-3600>' under vpc domain for anycast gateway SVIs
Tune NVE 'source-interface hold-down-timer <1-1500>' (default 180s) — MUST be longer than delay restore
Tune NVE 'source-interface hold-down-timer <1-1500>' (default 180s) — MUST be longer than delay restore
Tune NVE 'source-interface hold-down-timer <1-1500>' (default 180s) — MUST be longer than delay restore
On NX-OS 10.4.1+: consider split loopbacks with 'fabric-ready time' for faster orphan convergence

On NX-OS 10.4.1+: consider split loopbacks with 'fabric-ready time' for faster orphan convergence

root_causes.cause: 'delay restore' timer too short (default 150s) for underlay/overlay convergence

Orphan port delay-restore not configured
DAG SVI 'delay restore interface-vlan' too short (default 10s)

failure_modes.description: During migration from classic Ethernet (HSRP) to VXLAN EVPN (Distributed Anycast Gateway), the border leaf's DAG SVI sends ARP requests using the anycast gateway MAC/IP, but ARP replies are consumed by the HSRP SVI, resulting in incomplete ARP entries and connectivity failure.

resolutions.action: Upgrade to NX-OS 10.2(3) or later which supports HSRP/DAG coexistence
Upgrade to NX-OS 10.2(3) or later which supports HSRP/DAG coexistence
Upgrade to NX-OS 10.2(3) or later which supports HSRP/DAG coexistence
Pre-migration workaround: align HSRP VMAC to DAG MAC ('mac-address 2020.0000.00aa' under HSRP group)
Pre-migration workaround: align HSRP VMAC to DAG MAC ('mac-address 2020.0000.00aa' under HSRP group)
Pre-migration workaround: align HSRP VMAC to DAG MAC ('mac-address 2020.0000.00aa' under HSRP group)
Use 'ip address <secondary> use-bia' on border leaf SVIs for proxy-ARP functionality
Use 'ip address <secondary> use-bia' on border leaf SVIs for proxy-ARP functionality
Use 'ip address <secondary> use-bia' on border leaf SVIs for proxy-ARP functionality
Migrate VLANs one at a time — do not run HSRP and DAG on same VLAN simultaneously on pre-10.2(3)
Migrate VLANs one at a time — do not run HSRP and DAG on same VLAN simultaneously on pre-10.2(3)
Migrate VLANs one at a time — do not run HSRP and DAG on same VLAN simultaneously on pre-10.2(3)

root_causes.cause: HSRP and DAG cannot coexist on the same SVI prior to NX-OS 10.2(3)
ARP reply consumed by HSRP SVI before reaching DAG processing
HSRP VMAC not aligned to DAG MAC (2020.0000.00aa)

messages.description: Synchronizes MAC tables, IGMP snooping, ARP tables, HSRP state, and STP management between vPC peers over peer-link. In vPC Fabric Peering mode, CFS runs over the VXLAN fabric and requires DSCP-based QoS prioritization on spine switches.
key_fields: Message Type (MAC sync, IGMP sync, ARP sync, HSRP sync, STP sync, Config sync)
messages.name: CFS (Cisco Fabric Services)
messages.when_sent: Continuously over peer-link for state synchronization

messages.description: UDP heartbeat between vPC peers for dual-active detection
messages.name: Peer-Keepalive

purpose: Virtual Port-Channel — allows a pair of Cisco Nexus switches to appear as a single logical switch for LACP and STP

states.description: Switch elected as vPC primary. Continues forwarding on all vPC member ports and orphan ports under normal operation.
states.name: PRIMARY

states.description: Switch elected as vPC secondary. Forwards traffic identically to the primary under normal operation.
states.name: SECONDARY

states.description: Primary switch with secondary peer down. Primary continues forwarding but split-brain risk exists if peer is actually still active (prolonged state indicates a failure).
states.name: PRIMARY_SECONDARY_DOWN

states.description: Secondary switch with primary peer down. Secondary takes over primary role and continues forwarding to maintain connectivity.
states.name: SECONDARY_PRIMARY_DOWN

states.description: Peer-link has failed but peer-keepalive is still active. The secondary switch suspends its vPC member ports to prevent dual-active; only the primary continues forwarding.
states.name: PEER_LINK_DOWN

timers.description: Interval between UDP keepalive messages between vPC peers
timers.name: peer-keepalive-interval
timers.tuning_advice: Default is appropriate for most deployments

timers.description: Time to wait for keepalive before declaring peer dead
timers.name: peer-keepalive-timeout
timers.on_expiry: Peer declared unreachable; influences dual-active detection

timers.description: Time after reload before vPC auto-recovery activates (allows orphan port traffic when both peers were down)
timers.name: auto-recovery-reload-delay
timers.on_expiry: vPC auto-recovery activates; switch brings up vPCs even without peer
timers.tuning_advice: Enable for environments where both switches may reload simultaneously

transitions.trigger: Peer-link and peer-keepalive both lost from secondary peer
Peer-link and peer-keepalive both lost from primary peer
Secondary peer recovered (peer-keepalive and peer-link restored)

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Secondary will suspend its vPC member ports; only primary continues forwarding
transitions.trigger: Peer-link failed but peer-keepalive still active
Peer-link failed but peer-keepalive still active

transport: CFS over IP (peer-link) and peer-keepalive (UDP/3200 by default)

notes.note: vPC is NX-OS specific. IOS-XE uses StackWise Virtual (SVL) for a similar dual-switch single-logical concept on Catalyst 9000.
vPC is NX-OS specific. IOS-XR uses MC-LAG (Multi-Chassis LAG) for dual-homing.

vPC requires 'feature vpc'. Configure with 'vpc domain <id>', peer-keepalive, and peer-link. vPC auto-recovery and peer-gateway are recommended best practices. Type-1 consistency checks are enforced (mismatch suspends vPC). 'peer-gateway' needed when downstream device sends traffic with HSRP MAC as destination.
vPC+ extends vPC into FabricPath domains. For VXLAN fabrics, vPC is still used at the leaf layer with EVPN multi-homing as an alternative.

CRITICAL: vPC domain IDs must be unique across all vPC pairs in a contiguous L2 domain. The system-MAC is auto-derived from the domain ID and used in STP BPDUs, LACP, and IGMP. Duplicate domain IDs cause MAC collisions and unpredictable forwarding.

vPC role election: lower priority wins (default 32667). If priority ties, lower system MAC wins. Election is non-preemptive by default — role sticks after election. 'role priority <1-65535>' under vpc domain to influence.

In vPC VXLAN EVPN: 'system nve infra-vlans <vlan-list>' designates VLANs carried over peer-link for infrastructure purposes (backup routing, BUM traffic transfer). These VLANs must be trunked on the peer-link. Missing infra-VLANs causes silent forwarding failures.

vPC Fabric Peering (virtual peer-link): Runs peer-link over VXLAN fabric through spines instead of dedicated front-panel ports. Requires 'virtual peer-link destination <IP> source <IP> dscp 56', port-channel with no physical members, and 'port-type fabric' on uplinks. CFS traffic MUST be classified by DSCP and placed into strict priority class on spines — without this, CFS may be dropped under congestion.

With 'advertise-pip', Type-5 (IP prefix) routes use the individual PIP as next-hop instead of shared VIP. Type-2 (MAC/IP) routes still use VIP. Without advertise-pip, all routes use VIP, which can cause unnecessary traffic through the peer-link for inter-subnet routes.

In vPC VXLAN orphan port handling: Type-2 and Type-5 routes for orphan ports use the PIP (Primary IP), not the VIP. NX-OS 10.4.1 introduces 'split loopbacks' optimization with 'fabric-ready time' for faster orphan port convergence after peer reload.

vPC L3 backup routing over peer-link: Use a dedicated transit VLAN with point-to-point routing adjacency (OSPF/EIGRP). All other FHRP SVIs should be 'passive-interface' to prevent routing adjacencies from forming over the peer-link on every SVI.
